factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Strong May NFP report (172K jobs vs 85K expected) has triggered hawkish Fed repricing; bond market now pricing 25bps rate hike in 2026. Chair Warsh faces policy tests with Trump wanting lower rates but data supporting tighter policy.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge
factors.summary: 10-year and 2-year yields rocketed higher post-NFP (2-year +12bps, 10-year +6bps); elevated yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow components (UNH, utilities, REITs) and increase discount rate on equity valuations.

factors.name: Tech/Chip Sector Selloff
factors.summary: NASDAQ suffered worst day since April 2025 (down 4.18%, -1,121 points on record), with chip stocks (Marvell, Micron) collapsing. Dow's tech/industrial exposure (MSFT, BA) faces spillover pressure. Meta considering tens of billions equity raise signals capital dilution concerns.

factors.name: Broad Equity Selloff Momentum
factors.summary: S&P 500 down 2.65%, wiping $1.8 trillion. Friday's selloff accelerated into close; Dow itself down 1.35% but lagging the broader indices suggests defensive rebalancing into dividend names—temporary support but fragile.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallied to ~100.10 post-NFP; strong dollar pressures multinational earnings translation (Dow has ~50% international revenue). Dow heavyweights like CAT, BA, GE face headwinds on FX conversion.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Escalation
factors.summary: US strikes on Iranian radar sites, Gulf flare-up, and Houthi threats to Bab el-Mandeb Strait raise oil/energy volatility. Risk-off sentiment depresses cyclicals; Trump's claim Iran talks 'going well' offers limited credibility given active military engagement.

factors.name: Earnings Season Resilience Question
factors.summary: News headline 'resilient, but for how long?' captures investor hesitation. Strong jobs data now raises wage/margin compression concerns; Dow heavyweights face cost-push pressure ahead of next earnings cycle.

factors.name: Dow-Specific Composition Risk
factors.summary: Price-weighted index means MSFT, UNH, GS, BA tech/financial exposure gets hit hard in risk-off; industrials (CAT, BA) already weakening on strong USD and geopolitical uncertainty. No offsetting strength visible in names like JNJ (beat talc lawsuit but limited upside driver).

factors.name: Credit/Liquidity Stress Signals
factors.summary: No major credit spread blowout yet, but VIX likely elevated post-NFP (not quoted in data). Tech mega-cap equity raises (Google $85B, Meta considering billions) signal capital-raising desperation; not an immediate credit crisis but dilution pressure on valuations.

factors.name: Trump Administration Policy Uncertainty
factors.summary: Tariff uncertainty, wind energy project cancellations, and pardon for insider-trading former congressman create mixed signals. Trump wants lower rates but Warsh/Fed agenda increasingly hawkish; policy conflict unresolved.

screening.narrative: The Dow sold off 1.35% Friday as a stronger-than-expected May jobs report (172K vs 85K) triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate-hike expectations, pushing Treasury yields sharply higher and killing momentum in tech/growth stocks—the NASDAQ's worst day since April 2025. While the Dow is price-weighted and less exposed to the hardest-hit chip names, its multinational industrial and financial components (MSFT, CAT, GS, BA) are under pressure from dollar strength, elevated borrowing costs, and geopolitical risk (Iran/Gulf escalation). The selloff is in early-to-mid phase: Friday's decline is fresh but conviction needs confirmation Monday. Earnings season rhetoric ('resilient, but for how long?') reflects investor anxiety about margin compression and capital-raising dilution (Google, Meta) ahead.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Warsh public remarks on rate policy (Trump wants cuts; data supports hikes—policy divergence risk)
ISM Manufacturing PMI & Services PMI release (next week) — key for confirming industrial slowdown vs. labor resilience
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production (next week) — Dow-sensitive cyclical signals
Dow heavyweight earnings revisions and guidance (Q2 forward guidance) — margin compression risk on wage/energy inflation
Oil/energy price action (Brent crude sensitivity to Houthi/Iran escalation) — direct impact on CVX, XOM, and transportation costs
Further tech earnings and capital-raise announcements (MSFT, GOOGL capex guidance) — dilution/valuation concerns
China PMI data (if released this week) — demand signal for Caterpillar, Boeing materials/exports
screening.risk_warning: If Fed officials signal dovishness or pivot away from rate-hike pricing mid-week, yields roll over and equities rebound—particularly tech/growth. If Iran talks genuinely de-escalate or Houthi threats recede, geopolitical risk premium deflates. If upcoming earnings prove resilient despite strong labor data (pointing to productivity gains not wage spiral), the Dow could stabilize and retest resistance. A surprise negative economic data release (ISM, jobless claims) would flip the bias to BUY quickly. Dow's dividend-heavy nature means sharp yield spikes can also trigger rebalancing pain but later attract value buyers.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (Friday's selloff fresh but underway). Do not chase shorts into the close Monday; wait for Dow to test 50,500–50,650 support and establish whether Friday's decline holds or bounces. If support fails cleanly, enter shorts on retest of 50,800–50,900. If support holds and yields stabilize, await ISM/earnings data before committing. Current -1% intraday losses suggest further downside risk but phase 2 chop is likely—patience favors defined-entry over emotional chasing.

factors.name: Strong NFP / Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg, MarketWatch), driving hawkish Fed repricing. KPMG Chief Economist noted bond markets now pricing 25bp rate hike in 2026, a significant shift from prior easing expectations.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge
factors.summary: 10-year yield surged +6bp, 2-year +12bp following NFP (FXStreet). Higher rates pressure rate-sensitive Dow components (financials initially benefit but equity multiples compress; industrials face higher cost of capital).

factors.name: Tech Sector Collapse
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.18% worst day since April 2025, S&P 500 -2.65%. Semiconductor stocks crashed hardest in 6 years (MarketWatch). Although Dow is less tech-heavy, contagion effect and risk-off sentiment weighs on all indices.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Equity Dilution Fears
factors.summary: Meta considering tens of billions in stock offering following Google's $85B equity sale (Financial Times via multiple sources). Signals AI capex strain and potential sector-wide dilution trend affecting MSFT (Dow component).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk - Iran Conflict
factors.summary: US attacked Iranian radar sites after drone launches (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg). Ongoing Gulf tensions increase supply chain/energy risks for Dow multinationals despite Trump claiming Iran talks 'going well'.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallied to near 100.10 from 99.16 low post-NFP (FXStreet). Dow has ~50% international revenue exposure; stronger dollar creates translation headwinds for multinational earnings.

factors.name: Momentum / Retail Investor Fatigue
factors.summary: Bloomberg noted 'Tech Selloff Tests Retail Investor Strength' - questioning durability of retail bid. Bitcoin crashed below $60K to Oct 2024 lows, signaling broader risk-off in momentum trades.

factors.name: Earnings Season Assessment
factors.summary: Stock Market News headline 'Earnings season takeaways: resilient, but for how long?' suggests earnings held up but forward uncertainty rising. No major Dow component earnings in news cycle to provide concrete directional signal.

factors.name: Boeing Production News
factors.summary: Boeing studying boost to 737 MAX production to highest-ever rate, new assembly line starting July 6. Marginally positive for BA but offset by broader market headwinds and already known production ramp expectations.

factors.name: Sector Rotation
factors.summary: Goldman's Flood sees buying opportunity but notes S&P 500 selloff reached -3% intraday. Dow down only -1.35% vs NASDAQ -4.18% suggests relative outperformance, but still in confirmed downtrend with no reversal signals yet.

confirmation.narrative: Dow is in confirmed bearish phase following stronger-than-expected May NFP (172K vs 85K est) which triggered aggressive repricing of Fed policy toward potential 2026 rate hike. Treasury yields spiked (2Y +12bp, 10Y +6bp), pressuring equity valuations across the board. While Dow's -1.35% decline is modest vs NASDAQ's -4.18% collapse, the index faces headwinds from surging dollar (multinational earnings translation), rising rate environment (cost of capital for industrials), geopolitical risk (Iran conflict), and contagion from tech sector equity dilution fears (Meta/Google capital raises). Current -1.02% 24h move indicates Phase 2 - initial reaction underway but not fully extended.
catalysts_ahead: Next Fed meeting decision (June FOMC if scheduled)
China PMI/growth data (affects CAT, BA, materials demand)
CPI/PPI releases (input cost pressures for industrials)
Dow component earnings (particularly MSFT given AI capex scrutiny)
Iran conflict developments (oil price/supply chain implications)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed officials push back on rate hike expectations in next 48h, (2) 10Y yield reverses below 4.3% (if currently ~4.4%+), (3) Iran de-escalation headline catalyst, (4) Dow reclaims 51,200 resistance on strong volume. Goldman noting 'buying opportunity' suggests institutional bid may emerge at lower levels around 50,400 support.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (Phase 2) after NFP catalyst; wait for bounce to 51,000-51,200 resistance zone to enter shorts, or confirm breakdown below 50,400 support for continuation. Avoid chasing current levels.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: Strong May NFP (172K vs 85K expected)
factors.summary: Better-than-expected jobs report (172K vs 85K consensus per MarketWatch/Bloomberg, 2026-06-05T20:39) pushes Fed rate-hike expectations forward, lifting Treasury yields sharply (+12bps 2Y, +6bps 10Y per FXStreet). Dow's dividend payers and rate-sensitive industrials face margin compression.

factors.name: 10-year Treasury yields surging
factors.summary: Yields rocketing higher following hot NFP (per FXStreet 2026-06-05T19:21); rate-sensitive Dow heavyweights (UNH, JPM, GS financials, utilities) face valuation headwinds. Rising cost of capital pressures M&A and capex guidance.

factors.name: Tech/Big Cap equity funding squeeze
factors.summary: Google sold $85B equity, Meta considering tens of billions more for AI capex (MarketWatch 2026-06-05T21:15); large secondary issuance supply depresses sentiment. Nasdaq down 4.18% on Friday (worst day since April 2025), signaling risk-off rotation affecting Dow's tech-heavy weighting.

factors.name: Nasdaq selloff (biggest point drop on record, -1,121 pts)
factors.summary: Nasdaq composite tumbled 4.18% Friday with biggest daily point loss on record (MarketWatch 2026-06-05T22:27); semiconductor sector collapsed (worst day in 6 years per MarketWatch 2026-06-05T21:03). Dow lagged at -1.35% Friday but faces spillover Monday.

factors.name: Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran strikes)
factors.summary: US struck Iranian drones/radar; Iran targeted US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain (BBC/Al Jazeera 2026-06-06T06:16). Red Sea shipping risk (Bab el-Mandeb) threatens oil supply, raising cost-of-goods pressures on industrials. Uncertainty weighs on risk appetite.

factors.name: Credit/Private credit cooling
factors.summary: Private credit boom slowing sharply on lending/flows pullback (All News 2026-06-06T06:13); Raizen $13B restructuring (Bloomberg 2026-06-05T23:31) signals credit stress. Tighter financial conditions penalize cyclical capex/M&A.

factors.name: Dollar strength post-NFP
factors.summary: USD rallied to ~100.10 post-NFP (FXStreet 2026-06-05T21:22); Dow has ~50% international revenue — stronger dollar translates foreign earnings lower. No hedge in place; earnings revisions downward likely.

factors.name: China growth concerns + regulatory tightening
factors.summary: China tightening oversight of private investment funds (All News 2026-06-06T06:14); Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand tied to China PMI near stall. SpaceX facing China website ban (All News 2026-06-06T06:08) signals geopolitical decoupling headwind.

factors.name: Austrian sovereign downgrade
factors.summary: Austria lost last top-tier credit rating due to persistent fiscal deficits (Bloomberg 2026-06-05T05:59); signals broader developed-market credit fatigue. European demand for US exports weakens if EU falters.

factors.name: Prior bias alignment (SELL 7/10 from 2026-06-06 02:57)
factors.summary: Haiku and Sonnet both flagged SELL/BEARISH 7/10 as of early 06-06; fresh selloff validates negative bias. Continuation likely into Monday unless late-day reversal Friday invalidates.

screening.narrative: Friday's strong May NFP (172K jobs vs. 85K expected) triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate hike odds, lifting 10Y Treasury yields +6bps and 2Y yields +12bps. Tech selloff cascaded through equities (Nasdaq down 4.18%, worst day since April 2025; Marvell, Micron, semiconductor complex crushed), dragging Dow -1.35% in a flight-to-safety rotation. Compounding bearish drivers: Big Tech equity capital raises (Google $85B, Meta tens of billions) flood secondary supply; geopolitical flare-up (US-Iran strikes) risks oil shocks; China regulatory tightening and private credit cooling signal demand weakness. Dow's dividend yields look less attractive as real yields rise; multinational earnings face FX headwinds from a stronger dollar. Phase 2 selloff is now underway, with Monday's open likely to test 50,500–50,200 support unless Fed pivot signals emerge.
catalysts_ahead: Monday 2026-06-09: Likely gap-down open if Asia markets extend Friday's tech rout; watch for any weekend geopolitical escalation
Week of 2026-06-09: ISM Manufacturing PMI (early week) — critical for Dow's industrial weighting; miss expectations would worsen selloff
Week of 2026-06-09: ISM Services PMI, factory orders, durable goods — real-economy hard data to validate/invalidate hot jobs report signal
2026-06-16–2026-06-18 (approx): FOMC expectations reset; if Powell signals hawkish hold, equity weakness extends; dovish pivot could trigger short-covering rally
Ongoing: Fed speaker calendar for rate-hike guidance; any 50bps hike talk would trigger fresh selloff
China PMI/economic data (if released mid-week): validate or refute growth slowdown narrative
screening.risk_warning: Risk to this SELL bias: (1) if Fed Chair Warsh or FOMC speakers signal pause/pivot to dovish language, real yields compress and equities bounce hard—watch for any 'we've done enough' commentary; (2) if US-Iran tension de-escalates abruptly or ceasefire agreed, oil dips and risk appetite recovers; (3) if Monday's US market open is stabilizing (limited gap-down, positive reversal), this may mark exhaustion of Phase 2 move, requiring pullback-entry thesis rather than fresh shorts; (4) strong ISM Manufacturing or durable goods could support Dow cyclicals and validate the jobs report as non-inflationary, triggering re-rating higher.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (underway, ~24h after catalyst). Do not chase shorts here. Instead, identify retest levels: if Dow bounces to 51,000–51,100 on Monday/Tuesday, consider re-short with tight stop above 51,200. Watch for support at 50,500; if it breaks convincingly, next target 50,200–50,000. Alternatively, wait for ISM Manufacturing (early week) to confirm continued economic stress before adding size—premature entry risks whipsaw into Fed-dovish surprise.
